title: Dog Back Leg Paralysis: Causes and Emergency Care Guide
meta_desc: If your dog suddenly cannot stand or has paralyzed back legs, it is a high-priority emergency. Learn about IVDD and the next steps for recovery.
content_html:
What is Sudden Hind Limb Paralysis?

Sudden loss of function in the back legs, often referred to as paraparesis or paraplegia, occurs when the communication between the brain and the hind limbs is interrupted. This is most frequently caused by issues within the spinal cord. Common conditions include Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD), spinal trauma, or a Fibrocartilaginous Embolism (FCE), which is essentially a stroke in the spine.

Is This a Medical Emergency?

Yes. This is a high-urgency medical emergency. If your dog is unable to support their weight, is dragging their back legs, or is showing signs of extreme pain (shivering, panting, or vocalizing), you must seek veterinary care immediately. Delaying treatment can lead to permanent nerve damage or the loss of deep pain perception, which significantly lowers the chances of a successful recovery.

How a Photo or Video Helps Triage

While you are preparing to transport your dog, capturing a short video of their movement (or attempt to move) can be incredibly helpful for the veterinary triage team. A video allows the doctor to see the exact nature of the gait, whether there is "knuckling" (walking on the tops of the paws), or if there is total loss of motor function. This visual data helps the medical team prioritize your pet's case the moment you walk through the clinic doors.

faqs.question: Can a dog recover from back leg paralysis?
faqs.answer: Yes, many dogs regain the ability to walk. Recovery depends on the underlying cause, the severity of the nerve damage, and how quickly medical or surgical intervention is provided.

faqs.question: What is the most common cause of sudden paralysis?
faqs.answer: Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD) is the most common cause, especially in breeds with long backs like Dachshunds, Corgis, and French Bulldogs.

faqs.question: How should I move my dog if they can't walk?
faqs.answer: Keep the spine as neutral as possible. Use a flat board or a thick, supportive blanket as a stretcher to prevent further spinal shifting during transport.
